demographics.living_situation: lives at home with both parents

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: The feeding pump disconnected overnight, he wouldn't wake up this morning, and then his arms started shaking violently. (Parent calling on behalf of their 8-year-old son.)
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: His overnight tube feed came off in the night, this morning we couldn't wake him properly, he had a short shaking episode, and now he's confused and his arms and legs are cramping badly. We need help now.

symptom_narrative.onset: noticed this morning on waking, after the overnight feeding tube came off in the night about 6 hours ago
symptom_narrative.duration: since he woke up — worsening over the last hour or so
symptom_narrative.character: he was floppy and wouldn't wake properly, then had a short episode of his whole body shaking with his eyes not responsive for about half a minute; now he's awake but muddled and his arms and legs are cramping
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: the parents say it is the most frightening thing they have seen with him and he seemed 'not there' during the shaking
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: the cramping is in his calves and forearms; the confusion and floppiness are all over
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: came on after a night without his feed; the shaking was one brief episode, the cramping and confusion are ongoing
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: nothing yet — the parents are frightened and rang straight away
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: missing his overnight feed is what set it off
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: pale, sweaty and clammy, breathing fast, very tired and slow to answer
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: he has had low blood sugars before if a feed is missed, but never a shaking episode like this
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: normally walks and plays normally; right now he is stuck in bed, too cramped and confused to move

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: a glycogen storage disease — the parents say his liver cannot store and release sugar properly, so his blood sugar drops if he doesn't eat continuously
past_medical_history.how_managed: continuous overnight feed through a tube into his stomach, plus uncooked cornstarch mixtures during the day to keep his sugar up

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: he has always been small for his age and has a noticeably big/swollen tummy
past_medical_history.how_managed: followed by a children's metabolic specialist team

current_medications_as_reported.name_as_patient_states: uncooked cornstarch mixture through the day

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: 8-year-old cared for at home by both parents, who manage his overnight tube feeds; both parents are present and made the call

paediatric_context.developmental_concerns: documented as small for age / growth restricted, followed by a paediatric metabolic team

objective_data_offered.value: home glucometer read 2.1 mmol/L when we checked him this morning
objective_data_offered.timing: this morning, around when he woke and before any sugar was given
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: single fingerprick on the family's home glucometer; not a laboratory value

objective_data_offered.value: child is pale and sweaty, lying floppy in bed, slow to answer; visible twitching/cramping of the calves and forearms; abdomen looks large and distended
he is breathing fast and a bit deep, is muddled and drowsy, and had one brief whole-body shaking episode about 30 seconds long earlier
the overnight feeding tube is disconnected next to the bed; the cornstarch mixture is on the kitchen counter
